references.1: Started with Tkinter turtle, then PyGame, then HTML5 Canvas. Returned to Python with much more ambitious ideas in AI.
references.2: Built VLMs, MLMs, SLMs. Improved LLMs. Really deep diving in VLMs right now.
references.3: Multi-step agent systems with memory, iterative feedback, RAG & domain-specific constraints — e.g. Osler for hospitalists, Aura for enterprise.
references.4: Studying deformables, not just sim. Also doing generative serial chains as a side project.
references.5: Must-know skill. I practice this in all work.
references.6: 2nd year. Most skills still come from outside the classroom.
references.7: Autonomous multi-floor delivery robots; elevator navigation, payload delivery. Backed by Founders Inc., based in SF.
references.8: Clinical transformers, PK/PD modeling, S3-backed pipelines. NYC.
references.9: Collaborative AI internal knowledge platform; reached 600 users over summer 2025.
references.10: Never took a tutorial. What I've made is what I know.
references.12: Updating irregularly since summer 2021 — AI (tech & philosophy), programming & life.
references.13: Completely offline. Mainly Camelback in the Poconos.
references.14: There's something satisfying about building things with your own hands.
references.15: White rice, black beans, chicken al pastor ×2, tomatillo red chili, roasted chili corn, cheese, light sour cream.
